title: Today's AI Brief
summary: The Vatican invites Anthropic to the Pope's AI encyclical presentation, and we dive into the world of AI agents and their impact on the tech world.
imageAlt: Image from source article for Why the Vatican Invited Anthropic to the Pope’s AI Encyclical Presentation
imageCaption: Why the Vatican Invited Anthropic to the Pope’s AI Encyclical Presentation
imageSourceTitle: Why the Vatican Invited Anthropic to the Pope’s AI Encyclical Presentation

body.text: I am looking at a fascinating story about the Vatican inviting Anthropic, a prominent AI company, to the Pope's AI encyclical presentation. This move signals an unprecedented alliance between the Catholic church and Silicon Valley. But what's behind this partnership? According to Wired, the Vatican's interest in Anthropic stems from the company's work on AI safety and transparency.

Let's take a look at some of the other news items that caught my attention. Rethinking organizational design in the age of agentic AI is a topic that's gaining traction. A recent article in Technology Review highlights the disconnect between ambition and execution when it comes to adopting AI agents in the enterprise. It seems that while many organizations want to be 'agentic' within the next three years, their current operations and infrastructure can't support that change.

Another story that stood out to me is about how AI agents plunged the tech world into chaos. It's a wild tale of techies getting swept up by coding tools like Anthropic's Claude. I mean, who wouldn't want to attend a meetup called Claude Code Anonymous? It sounds like a support group for people who've become too attached to their AI-powered coding tools.

What's interesting about these stories is how they highlight the complexities of AI adoption. On one hand, we have the Vatican partnering with Anthropic to explore the potential benefits of AI. On the other hand, we have stories about AI agents causing chaos in the tech world. It's a reminder that AI is a double-edged sword – it has the potential to bring about tremendous benefits, but it also requires careful consideration and planning.

I also came across a story about the economics of AI model development. It's a topic that's not often discussed, but it's crucial to understanding the AI landscape. As AI models become more complex and sophisticated, the cost of developing and maintaining them increases. This raises important questions about who will bear the cost of AI development and how we can ensure that AI benefits are shared equitably.

As I reflect on these stories, what stands out to me is the sheer pace of change in the AI world. It's like trying to drink from a firehose – there's so much happening, and it's hard to keep up. But amidst all the chaos, I think there's a deeper conversation to be had about what we want AI to achieve and how we can ensure that it benefits humanity as a whole. That's a topic for another time, but for now, I'm just going to sit back and enjoy the ride.
